key_points: The Earth rotates on its axis once every 24 hours causing day and night
The Earth revolves around the Sun in about 365 days causing seasons
The Moon revolves around the Earth and also rotates on its axis
Different phases of the Moon occur because we see different portions of the Moon illuminated by the Sun
The Sun is the main source of energy for the Earth
examples: Day and night cycle happens due to Earth's rotation
Seasons occur due to the Earth's revolution around the Sun
Phases of the Moon include new moon, half moon and full moon
Solar energy supports life on Earth
formulas: Revolution period of Moon around Earth → about 27 days
exam_tips: Understand how Earth's rotation and revolution affect life on Earth
Remember the sequence of Moon phases
Use diagrams to explain Earth–Sun–Moon relationships
Write clear explanations for day-night and seasonal changes
